string.text: Sorry, I'm really pressed for time right now.
Hey, stalker! How are you doing?
Hello. I don't believe we've met before... Though, I must admit, my memory for faces isn't very good. What's your name?
Hello. My name is Marked One. Are you sure we haven't met before? The thing is, I've lost my memory and now I'm looking for anyone who might know me...

Is that so? I sympathize, Marked One... No, I don't recall us ever meeting. So, is that all you're interested in? Perhaps you have artifacts to sell or are ready to share valuable information in our field of work?
I'm mostly just looking around for now. Do you pay well for artifacts? And can I get some gear in exchange?

Yes, we have some items for exchange, primarily medical supplies, provisions, and ammunition. Bring us materials useful for our research, and then we'll open a special account for you to handle our mutual settlements. The account currency is conditional. Let's say, some sort of "transferable rubles". The exchange rate will depend on the value of what you bring.
Understood. Let's see how that works out.
Hello, um... Marked One? Did you want something? Perhaps you have some information for us, or do you want to sell artifacts?
Marked One, is it you? How can I help?
You told me about the timing to get into laboratory X-16, but how exactly do I get in? Where is the entrance?

Vasiliev was handling the laboratory issue for us. About a week ago, he led a small group in an attempt to infiltrate it. A few hours later, we felt a decent tremor, and the radiation disappeared for a while, and an hour after that, we received a signal from Vasiliev. The signal came from the swamp behind our camp, but soon vanished. By all appearances, everyone died. Vasiliev definitely knew how to get inside, but... Try to find his body. It's possible he kept some notes and they are still on him.
Anything else? Everything seems somewhat vague...

I really don't know what else to advise you. You should definitely start by searching for Vasiliev's body. The rest of his group - colleague Matveev and two stalker-guides - are unlikely to have made it out of the laboratory. We didn't register any signs of other people's presence when we received Vasiliev's signal. Oh, and here's a curious detail: it seemed to me that one of the stalkers, nicknamed Ghost, knew significantly more about X-16 than he let on. I admit he might have had some special interest in this expedition, and if by some miracle he survived... Though, of course, hoping for that is foolish.
Understood. Well, I'll start looking.
Well, my respected friend, did you manage to find the remains of anyone from Vasiliev's group?
Not yet. I'm working on it...

How should I put it... I found a hazmat suit, practically intact, though later there was some sort of flash and it disappeared... But before that, I managed to find a PDA in the suit's pocket, some notes from which I was able to read and even listen to. Ghost was mentioned there, and there was also something about you... And also about another scientist, as I understand - Ivan Sergeevich. Do you know him?
Then I wish you success. Anything else?

Hmm. It's been quite a while, after all. Apparently, there are no traces left, or maybe there never were any - who knows? Communication and telemetry work extremely unsteadily in the Zone. It could very well be that we recorded merely a phantom source, which wander the airwaves not infrequently. Their nature remains a mystery to us to this day.
So what now? A dead end? Are there no other options to find out where the entrance to the laboratory is?

I know Ivan Sergeevich, but we haven't seen each other in a long time. It seems to me he has a somewhat subjective approach to the subject of our research. As far as I know, he set up a temporary field research camp in the so-called Swamps.\nBut let's return to the main subject of your search. Did you find a body or... any hints as to where the owner of the suit went? And was there anything in the PDA about the entrance point to the laboratory?
There were no bodies, no other clues either. Just as there was no useful information about the entrance. What do you say, Professor?

This is all strange, very strange... I would advise you to visit colleague Grigorovich, Ivan Sergeevich. He, as far as I know, maintained fairly close contacts with Vasiliev. Perhaps they discussed the issue you're interested in, or he knows something from other sources.
So that means I have to go to the Swamps? Ugh, then I'll have to walk around with wet feet...
So that means I have to go to the Swamps? I haven't had the chance to be there yet. Could you point me in the right direction?
Yes, I agree, Ivan Sergeevich could have chosen a more suitable location for his expedition's base, but there's nothing to be done about it, as they say.
Unfortunately, I'm of no help to you here. I haven't inquired. Ask around among your peers. Also, I've heard there's an experienced trader at the Cordon, surely he must know...
Hello, Marked One. Are you here again regarding the expedition to the laboratory? I thought we had discussed everything... Or did something else bring you to us?

Marked One! I'm glad you managed to return alive and, more importantly, sane. Well, the path is known now. I think we will try to study what is located there, in those undergrounds, more closely. Unfortunately, our extremely tight funding framework does not allow us to properly reward this act, but the most valuable thing in this crazy modern world is information! We will, uh... "leak" to you, as is customary to say nowadays, a method for obtaining a mesomodif... in general, an altered artifact with new characteristics... or are you interested in something more material?
You phrased that nicely about the information. Leak away. Maybe it'll come in handy.
Information is great, of course... But my head is already spinning as it is. I'd prefer something more practical.
I dare hope you will be satisfied, Marked One. Thanks to brave people like you, we have the opportunity to try and put together this massive puzzle called the Zone. Thank you!

Will thirty thousand to your account suit you? By the look on your face, I see it does. Back in my institute years, when my classmates and I gathered in the evenings for a game of Preference, they told me I was a good physiognomist. However, I was never lucky at cards.\nUh... anyway, where was I? Yes! Thanks to brave people like you, we have the opportunity to try and put together this massive puzzle called the Zone. Thank you!
Hello, hello, Marked One. Glad to see you again! What brings you to us?
Marked One, you haven't shown up in a while. One moment you're dropping by almost every day, and the next you disappear for weeks.

Something happened out there... Could you tell me how it's possible that I spent a day, maybe two, at the Radar, but two weeks flew by? And all those "missing" days, except for that couple, left no trace in my memory.
Hmm. Did you experience delirious states? Hallucinations? Did you lose consciousness?

How should I know? The whole point is I don't remember losing consciousness or anything else. Well, I slept, of course. I'm starting to think, what if I was like a zombie all this time - passed out and walking around moaning like the others, and then something switched me back on. Do you think that's possible?

I cannot state for certain without knowing the full picture, but I feel that's a bit of a stretch. If what you say really took place, then you most likely experienced something akin to a coma or some sort of lethargic sleep. I don't think you were zombified. After all, if the zombified could have their consciousness "switched back on", it would mean the process is reversible and they could be cured. But that, unfortunately, is not the case. There is not a single piece of evidence.
Maybe I am the first piece of evidence?! And what does science say about time travel? Into the future, for example.

Let's not fantasize. I am, you know, not a science fiction writer, but a scientist, and as a scientist, I rely on facts. You haven't been here for a little over two weeks, and that is the only fact for me. Where you were and what you were doing, I do not know, but according to your words, only a couple of days passed for you. And those are no longer facts, but subjective data, highly unreliable at that, no offense. So let's drop the subject.
How are you, Marked One? I hope everything is fine after that incident with the "lost" time? What can I do for you?
Glad to see you, Marked One! What do you have to tell me?
I chatted with your colleague here. Kruglov is his last name. The man says smart things. Even knows Chinese, too. Why didn't you recommend I go straight to him about the translation?

What? What are you talking about? Ah... yes, forgive my forgetfulness. My head is always occupied with all sorts of things... Wait a minute... Was it you who helped Professor Kruglov reach our camp after his helicopter crashed? I just now put his story together and realized he was talking specifically about you.\nOn behalf of our entire scientific team, I would like to express our gratitude to you for this act!

What? What are you talking about? Ah... yes, forgive my forgetfulness. My head is always occupied with all sorts of things... Wait a minute... Was it you who helped Professor Kruglov recover his stolen research data? I just now put his story together and realized he was talking specifically about you.\nOn behalf of our entire scientific team, I would like to express our gratitude to you for this act!
What? What are you talking about? Ah... yes, forgive my forgetfulness. My head is always occupied with all sorts of things... So did you manage to solve that problem?
Come on, don't mention it. Different folks wander the Zone, sure, but honest stalkers respect scientists and always try to help... when possible.

My problems are entirely different, and this - is just life's little details. Though, Professor Kruglov, unlike you, kindly agreed to help me with the translation. Anyway, I won't bother you since you're always occupied with "all sorts of things"...
Nevertheless, I insist that you accept something from us as a token of appreciation. Here are some medical supplies. I'll also credit your personal account in our exchange fund with a certain amount.
Well... thank you, I wasn't expecting that, to be honest.
Hello. I'm looking for laboratory X-16 - I think that's what it's called. The Barkeep said it's somewhere around here, at Yantar, and that you might know its location.

Yes, we ourselves have been interested in laboratory X-16 for quite some time... Or rather, the device inside it, which generates radiation that primarily affects the cognitive functions of the human brain. If one tries to break through the conditional boundary, beyond which the degree of exposure begins to increase exponentially, without good protection and without pre-calculated extremum points, the result will inevitably be tragic. Even if you manage to avoid the hordes of zombies around the laboratory entrance, you risk turning into one of them. All of this is very serious. To carry out the calculations, however, we need the parameters of various psi-field variables at several fairly spatially separated points. If you help us with this, then, with some degree of probability, we can forecast the most favorable time to attempt penetrating the laboratory with minimal risk to you.
Can we make it simpler? Just tell me how I can help.

Forgive me, I was rambling... If you take measurements at the points we specify using a special device and deliver the results of these measurements to me, then we can calculate the time when it will theoretically be possible to access the factory territory without the danger of being zombified.
And the entrance to the laboratory is on the territory of this factory, I assume?

As far as I know, the entrance is in the central building. It's almost a certainty! But only a handful have managed to get inside so far. A great stroke of luck, I must say. Though what kind of luck is it when only mindless husks of people return...
Understood. Let me take the measurements and try to squeeze into this building.
Understood. I'm not ready to deal with this right now.
Your zeal is commendable! Get the device from colleague Semenov, and clarify the coordinates of the points where the measurements need to be taken.
Sakharov is asking for psi-field measurements around the factory at Yantar, in the locations marked on the map.
Hello, Professor. I'm here about the psi-field measurements in the vicinity of the local factory...
Well, well. Did you manage to get the data?
Well... I placed the device where I was told.
To be honest, I haven't gotten around to it yet. I thought I'd drop by and ask, just in case you figured it all out yourselves without me.
I beg your pardon?
No! It is absolutely impossible to do this without baseline data, the further approximation of which will be built on the field gradient indicators and the correlation with...
You said to measure at three points. I did that. At least the lights on the device lit up... here.
Alright, alright, I get it, there's no way around the measurements! Goodbye.
Yes, yes... At exactly three points. Let's see what we have there. Where is the recording device?
Here, take it.
Uhh... Where did I put it... Slight hiccup, I'll be right back, Professor.
Good, we'll check the readings and run them through a special program to get a forecast. Come back in about twenty minutes, I think everything will be ready by then.
Whatever you say.
Any news on the data I brought? Have you calculated when it's best to try and reach the lab entrance yet?
Yes, I just received the results of processing your measurements. In the near future, the intensity of the psychotropic radiation will decrease to an almost safe level at approximately
The computer hasn't finished calculating all the inputs yet. Be patient, Marked One.
And what does "to an almost safe level" mean?
No problem, I'll come back later.

It's quite simple. Giving guarantees in a place like the Zone means displaying an overly optimistic attitude, to put it mildly.\nHowever, the experience of previous measurements shows that the field intensity decreases approximately two hours before the extremum we calculated, and then begins to build up again. Also for about two hours. In this time frame, a person can enter the laboratory and return without suffering irreversible damage to the central nervous system. Provided, of course, that they aren't harmed by something or someone other than the unknown psi-radiation generator.
Alright, I got it. So, two hours before and two hours after the specified time.
Most importantly, don't mix up the time!
Yeah, no kidding. I should write it down, in case I actually forget.
I'm ready to try getting into the underground, but there's just one little thing...
Only one? You, my good man, are a desperate optimist. You're trying to accomplish something that not every suicidal person would choose... And just "one little thing". What is it?
A decent amount of time has passed since my measurements. Has anything changed? Maybe it's worth measuring again? Just to be safe.

Hmm... The thought, on one hand, is sound. But on the other... You could say you're very lucky: just the other day we conducted another routine series of measurements. Including at the very same points as you did. We had to ask representatives of the Duty faction to provide us with an armed escort. We've just finished processing the data. Everything indicates that the intensity of the psychotropic radiation will decrease to a conditionally safe level at approximately
Yeah, that worked out well. Also give or take a couple of hours, I assume?
Yes, yes, it decreases approximately two hours before the extremum we calculated, and then begins to build up again. Also for about two hours.
Right, got it. Thank you.
Professor, I received your message regarding the experiments with "Pendulums". But before we discuss that topic, please answer one question.
I'm listening, young man.
Is it somehow possible to replace data in the brain?

Ah, you're back on your usual topic... Well, with physical damage or, say, as a result of certain chemical reactions characteristic of the course of a number of diseases, some areas of the brain can lose neural connectivity with each other. In simple terms, access to information stored in isolated areas will be lost. All of this relates to organic tissue damage and can only cause data loss, but in no way the replacement of one set of data with another.
And if the brain isn't affected, is a substitution of consciousness, even partial, somehow possible? Say, an urge to perform certain actions, move in a specific direction... something like that.

Mm... I see where you're going with this. Of course, there are a number of purely psychological methods of influencing a person's consciousness in order to instill certain behavioral patterns in them. They can be used both with the application of chemical stimulants and without them. Suggestion, hypnosis, psychoprogramming, or psychocoding, as it's also called. Do you think something of this sort was done to you?

Whether it's that sort or not - I don't know. But I feel something itching inside me, keeping me awake and... It's like a call. Alluring and beckoning. You know, Professor, I was in Pripyat and saw strange people there, whom everyone calls Monolith members. They practically mistook me for one of their own. I spoke with their leader, although understanding him was difficult, he expressed himself so ornately...
Is that so? Very curious. And what did you talk to him about?

Well... About different things. You could say he helped me break through to the CNPP, but I'm not talking about that right now. All the Monolith members behaved very strangely. They were as if drugged... swaying in a trance or mumbling something unintelligible under their breath. And it just hit me: what if they're all programmed, as you put it? By the Monolith itself. And I'm actually just like them. It's just that my code is, well, in the initial stage of execution, or something...

A bold hypothesis! And you might be right, Marked One. Certain rumors have reached me... The nature of the mass psychosis among the current inhabitants of Pripyat defies logical explanation. On the other hand, after the so-called Brain Scorcher was disabled, the presence of anomalous radiation suppressing the will of people exposed to it was reliably recorded in the distant sectors of the Zone. A field of apathy - I believe that's what stalkers have dubbed this phenomenon. It emanated from somewhere in the area of the CNPP...

Exactly! Maybe prolonged exposure to this field once turned normal stalkers into Monolith psychos. And I, for instance, got a small dose of this "radiation", but managed to bail in time... What do you think, does that sound plausible enough? And if so, how do we purge this Monolith code from my consciousness?

I admit, I don't even know what to tell you. We need to know for certain on what principles the generation of such a field is based, but... I can concede that the result of prolonged exposure to intense wave radiation of a certain modulation could be...
Professor, please: simpler and shorter. I'm not interested in the scientific side of the issue. Only practical conclusions, please. This is very important to me.

Ahem... Then here's the thing: I admit that your assumption may turn out to be correct, but I don't know how you can get rid of the psychocoding. Although... wait a minute. If we're going to continue theorizing, let's return to why I invited you - "Pendulums". Do you remember our last conversation? You were asking about the possibility of interacting with the Monolith using this artifact.

Yes, of course. I haven't told you yet, but I even partially managed to practically test what you told me about back then. I found out a way to get "Pendulums", used it, and, being in the center of the Labyrinth, took in my hands... not even the artifact itself, but just the capsule with it, but even that was enough to soon recall one... notable episode. True, I didn't... uh... broadcast any mental message at that moment. But the result is undeniable, it couldn't have been a simple coincidence, I'm sure!

Incredible! Who would have thought that such a crazy theory would receive real confirmation! Yes, this gives hope that by an analogous method we can try to sever your connection to the Monolith, erase the psychocode, make you NOT marked. Well, well, this needs to be thought through...
... Professor?

Eh? Ah! We will need to do some preparatory work before I can develop a clear algorithm of actions for you. For starters, it would be a good idea to compile a complete psycho-matrix of your consciousness, and here we cannot do without outside help, since we simply do not have the necessary equipment. And they have it, as far as I know, in the camp of Professor Grigorovich, Ivan Sergeevich, and nowhere else in the Zone. You are acquainted with colleague Grigorovich, are you not?
Yes, of course. So I need to head to the Swamps? But will Ivan Sergeevich want to help me? Maybe you could draft some sort of "letter of recommendation" for him?

You see, Marked One, I wouldn't want to be indebted to him in any way. In our environment, people are very... jealous when it comes to the material support of scientific activities. Therefore, you shouldn't reference me, except perhaps in the most general terms. And I certainly won't openly ask Ivan Sergeevich for anything. This part of the plan lies entirely on you. I think you'll be able to find the right approach. If everything goes successfully, return here with the scan results of your consciousness's psycho-matrix. By that time, I hope, I'll have decided on the next steps.
Everything's so complicated with you, it turns out. Alright, we'll come to an agreement somehow, it's not my first time. See you later, Professor.

Had a long and unexpectedly productive conversation with Sakharov, after which I became confirmed in the idea that I need to try to get rid of the Monolith's "mark", its psychocode, as the professor puts it, which somehow ended up etched into my consciousness. The strange "Pendulum" artifact should help with this, but first I need to think everything through and prepare. I hope we haven't fantasized too much and all this won't be for nothing.
I managed to persuade your colleagues at the Swamps to do the necessary procedure. Well, that... recording of the psycho-matrix, or whatever it's called?!
Wonderful! I didn't doubt you'd manage to come to an agreement with Ivan Sergeevich. In what format did they give you the results?
Format? Ah... here, everything is on this flash drive.

I will immediately begin analyzing the psycho-matrix of your consciousness, and I suggest you tackle the next stage of preparation in the meantime. Remember I spoke about how important it is to select the right "key" for a specific type of interaction?
Of course I remember. I told you about the attempt to get my memory back following your recommendations. The Labyrinth anomaly acted as the key back then. I suppose it'll be something else this time?

You suppose correctly. I have a suitable location in mind. It's in the forest, which stalkers refer to exclusively as the Foggy Thicket. I'm aware that you once helped colleague Kruglov deliver memory cards from recording stations installed in various parts of the Zone, including the area of interest. So, the stations recorded the presence of a strange object in that sector, with a very characteristic energy background, similar to the one emitted by the unknown source from the CNPP area.

You suppose correctly. I don't know for sure yet, but... I have a candidate location in mind. It's in the forest, which stalkers refer to exclusively as the Foggy Thicket. To confirm my assumption, my friend, you will have to gather additional information about this area. Oh, don't worry, nothing complicated! By saying "gather", I mean literally collecting certain objects by hand, in the truest sense of the word.
This object is the "key"? Do you want me to go there and scout everything out myself? Do another scan of some incomprehensible crap with an unpronounceable name, did I guess right?
What objects? I don't understand anything...

Not this time. More precisely... it will also be necessary to conduct a scan, in order to have a complete picture of the "compatibility" of your psycho-matrix with the discovered object, but that's for later. First, a different task must be solved.\nThe fact is, young man, that the concentration of cross-psi-fields surrounding the object of interest is so high that our instruments lack the resolving power to reliably assess it. In practice, this leads to the emergence of a phenomenon such as the materialization of excess psi-energy, taking the form of so-called phantoms. Surely, during your wanderings through the Zone, you have repeatedly encountered something similar, but I'm certain not on such a scale...

Of course, I'll explain. Recently, we installed three measuring stations in different parts of the Foggy Thicket. Even though it's an outdated model, it's reliable and unpretentious, operating in automatic mode. Unfortunately, remote data retrieval from it is only provided via radio channel, not via satellite, as in more modern modifications. The connection is extremely unstable, some packets are lost during transmission, which is why we get a distorted picture. The only reliable option in this case is to use removable memory cards. Can you guess what I'm getting at?

You're talking about those sort of translucent figures of Zone mutants slowly flying through the air and passing through any obstacles? This plague happens to me regularly. Touching such a ghost feels like a firecracker going off in your head. A vile sensation, I must say. But they disappear perfectly fine from a hit with a regular bullet. So what's the problem? I crawled around in one underground once, and there were hordes of those phantoms there, and nothing - I'm alive, as you can see. I ran to some switch, pulled it, and it was all over.
You don't need to be a genius to guess. I have to pull the cards out of the devices and bring them to you. Right?

What are you saying?! Ah... I think I'm starting to guess... We came across documentation from one underground laboratory that mentioned an autonomous security system built on the principle of generating directed psi-streams. Only we didn't know that these streams took the form of phantoms familiar to every stalker. Highly curious analogies can be traced here. In one case, we are dealing with the natural, however strange it may sound, course of things, and in the other - with an artificial system. But both share a common basis, which means they must obey the same set of basic rules.
Absolutely right. I will mark the locations of the stations on your PDA. That's all, go, I need to focus.

I've already lost the thread of your reasoning, Professor. What does all this have to do with anything? Yes, I get that the point that needs to be checked might have a lot of phantoms, so what? I doubt they'll stop me from setting up your scanner.
Whatever you say.

Yes, but they will hinder its operation! We need to find a way to at least temporarily block the outflow of excess psi-energy in the required area. I confess, until you started talking about phantoms in the underground, I didn't have any special ideas, but now, I think I know what to do. Among the information that fell into our hands, there were no specifics regarding the operation of the security system, but there were references to a place where backup copies of the technical documentation for most of the experimental devices used in the network of underground laboratories are stored electronically. That's where the answer to our question might be found.

Backup copies, you say? A tasty morsel for you, isn't it? I understand, I understand. Even from this situation, you want to squeeze some personal benefit. I don't really care either way, I'm in regardless, as long as it leads to the intended goal. So where do I have to barge into this time, and what will I have to do there?

I value your practicality, Marked One. Your target is a server in the duty officer's room of the laboratory under index X-12. I'll warn you right away - I don't know where it is, or how you'll find the right room there.
That actually isn't a problem. I've been to that laboratory, and as for the room... I'll figure it out. So, should I drag the server back here to you, or maybe just take out the hard drive?

You never cease to amaze me, young man. You've managed to be everywhere already. I'd like to thoroughly question you about your adventures sometime, but I understand now is not the time.\nAs for the server, I have reason to believe that it is still in working condition and, being on the network, exchanges data with other machines. I wouldn't want to attract anyone's attention to the fact that one of the computers has stopped responding to requests. Take this flash drive, it contains a special program that will download all the information that might be of even the slightest interest by itself. All that is required of you is to plug it into the system unit, press any key on the keyboard, wait a few seconds, and then pull it out. If, however, I'm mistaken and it turns out the server isn't working, well, then I'll be satisfied if you bring its hard drive.
I got it all. You thought well with the flash drive. A reasonable precaution. See you later, Professor.
Professor, I was in the Foggy Thicket and gathered all the cards from your recorders.
Very good! Put them down right here...
Here, please. What now?
Now I need to read the information off them and run it through the analyzer program. It will take about three hours. I dare not keep you any longer.
I get the hint. I'll drop by in three hours.
Any news, Professor? Has your program finished analyzing the data from the Foggy Thicket cards?

Yes. I won't bore you with unnecessary details and will just say right away that my assumption about a suitable "key" received confirmation. The stations recorded the presence of a strange object in that sector, with a very characteristic energy background, similar to the one emitted by the unknown source from the CNPP area.
Not yet. I told you it would take about three hours.

And what are my next steps? Do you want me to personally go to the Thicket to look at this wondrous miracle? Do another scan of some incomprehensible crap with an unpronounceable name, did I guess right?
Yeah, right. I'll come by later, then.

Sakharov sent me to laboratory X-12, where I must find the duty officer's post, and download information from the server there onto a flash drive about the design of the security system generating phantoms in X-10. This knowledge should help come up with a way to block the similar "natural" phenomenon in the Foggy Thicket.
I downloaded the info from the server in X-12. Everything according to your instructions.

Astounding! The ones who designed the power supply for all these underground complexes knew their business well. The equipment is still in working condition, after so much time... Alright, hand over the flash drive. I can't wait to find out what secrets we've acquired.
Just please, first take a look and see what's useful for my case, and then study the rest...
Naturally, my dear friend, naturally. Give me a couple of hours to work with these files in peace.
Yes, of course. I'll drop by later.
I dropped by to check on the flash drive I brought. Did you find out how we can block the phantom outflow in the Foggy Thicket?

Imagine that, yes, I found out. That is, I want to say, I didn't particularly doubt the success, but still... I'm saying this so you don't think I sent you to that laboratory solely with the aim of partaking in other people's secrets.
Not so fast, young man. I haven't had the chance to look through all the documents yet. Please be so kind as not to distract me for now.

Professor, your motives are as clear as glacier water. The fact that you aren't helping me just for nothing is obvious. No need to try and look holier than the Pope, we have a mutually beneficial cooperation after all. So what do we do about the phantoms?
Pardon me. I'll check back later.

Uh... Yes, yes, you're right, of course.\nSo. Even though the security system of laboratory X-10 is an entirely man-made, artificial object, it turns out that for its functioning it requires - what do you think? - anomalous formations! Yes, you heard right, the system's principle of operation is based on stimulating the properties of several groups of artifacts using directed electromagnetic radiation. Surely you have many accompanying questions, but I'm not ready to discuss this topic in depth just yet.\nAs for our direct issue, the encapsulation of excess psi-energy within given boundaries is handled by a line of so-called "Ruby" artifacts: the "Ruby Spring", the "Ruby Film", and the "Ruby Battery".
Well, how about that... So that means I need to get my hands on three "rubies"? And what do I do with them then? And, strictly speaking, where? You still haven't named the exact location.

Ah, yes, the location. I'll send the geolocation to your tablet right now. Now, as to what to do with the artifacts... I have no certainty here, but considering the specifics of the energy picture in that area, I believe it will be sufficient to simply drop all the "rubies", as you called them, at the marked point. Have you had a chance to observe the collapsing effect of, for example, the "Springboard" anomaly as a result of interaction with a "Ruby Spring", no? I think an identical mechanism of mutual annihilation should work in our case.

Amazing! I think I'm gradually starting to make out this insane dialect of scientists... Hmm... I can guess what place you marked on my map. Some people call it the "Dead Monolith", by the way. You'd have to be a very paranoid person to believe in such a coincidence...\nSo, I throw out all three artifacts, the phantoms stop flying, and then... I believe the plan then moved on to the scan, right?

Amazing! I think I'm gradually starting to make out this insane dialect of scientists... Oh! Looks like a familiar spot, judging by the map. There's this huge crystal sticking out of the ground there, some people call it the "Dead Monolith", by the way. You'd have to be a very paranoid person to believe in such a coincidence...\nSo, I throw out all three artifacts, the phantoms stop flying, and then... I believe the plan then moved on to the scan, right?

Absolutely correct. Here's the device itself, take it. It is fully calibrated and ready for work. It's enough to simply place it at the point of interest and wait for the confirmation about the start of the scan, which will arrive on your PDA. If everything goes successfully, then by the time you return, we will already have received the first telemetry readings via satellite channel and processed them.\nAs for coincidences, personally I don't believe in them at all. Everything in our life is interconnected and natural, we just aren't always capable of tracking these connections. If the specified place is familiar to you, and on top of that it bears such a poetic name, that is a good sign. It means we can hope that all my assumptions regarding this issue have been correct so far. Well, Godspeed, as they say!
Yeah, onward and upward... Goodbye, Professor.

Thanks to the information extracted from X-12, it's become clear how to block the excess psi-energy in the Foggy Thicket, at the location that is potentially the "key" to establishing a connection with the Monolith. To do this, by analogy with the annihilation of certain anomalies using artifacts, it's required to drop a "Ruby Spring", "Ruby Battery", and "Ruby Film" at the point indicated by Professor Sakharov. After that, I must set up a special scanning device there to clarify the compatibility of my consciousness's psycho-matrix with this "key".

Professor, I visited the Foggy Thicket. It seems everything went according to plan. I dropped the three "ruby" artifacts near the "Dead Monolith", and the phantoms stopped flying. Then I set up the device in the same spot and waited for the message that the scan had started.

Yes, yes! We, in turn, just as I promised you, have already received the first results. I hasten to make you happy, Marked One: the compatibility of your consciousness's psi-matrix with this "key" is around 93.7%. I believe we won't find a more suitable place in the Zone.

Great! So, everything is ready for our "experiment" then? Now I can go to the "Dead Monolith" with the "Pendulum" and make a wish... well, I mean... you get the idea. Honestly, I have some doubts based on my experience from the previous "communication" with the Monolith, when I tried to get my memory back.

But the situation is fundamentally different now. Firstly, if I recall your story correctly, that time the operand was not the "Pendulum", but a capsule containing that artifact. Secondly, you were not properly focused and did not concentrate your mental signal. Thirdly, the "key-location" itself was chosen practically at random, and it's unknown to what extent it suited you. But even in such conditions—let's be frank, not the most favorable—you managed to get some kind of response. You must agree that we have prepared incomparably better now. However, the last factor remains unaccounted for, which, in my opinion, could have a significant impact on the course of the planned undertaking. I'm talking about the connection between the current phase of the "Pendulum" and the probable response. Do you remember our old conversation about the probability of the Zone disappearing as a phenomenon? I also mentioned that "Pendulums" can act as immensely powerful resonators...

Yes, I remember something like that. You also said then that it would require huge energy expenditures, and for that reason alone we shouldn't even dream of pulling off such a trick. But what does that have to do with this?

It has to do with the fact that resonance affects both objects. And even at incomparably lower energies, which will be involved during contact with the Monolith, there is a risk of receiving a "blowback", fatal to any living creature.
Well, here we are. What is the probability of such an outcome, and can it somehow be... minimized?

In order to answer your questions, I will need to conduct a series of experiments with the "Pendulum" artifact. And to rule out any calculation errors, the sample must be in perfect condition. You said you had information about a method for obtaining this artifact, correct? Then it won't be much trouble for you to close the final missing element in our, excuse the expression, puzzle.

In order to answer these questions, I had to conduct a series of experiments with the "Pendulum" artifact while you were gone. Fortunately, we had a suitable sample, which you yourself delivered to us from the helicopter crash site some time ago. I don't know if this specimen will be suitable for future research, as it clearly exhibits pronounced degradation of physical parameters... All in all, please appreciate this contribution to our common venture!\nBe that as it may, the results of the experiments will be ready any minute now - the program is already finishing the cross-calculation.
Some trouble that is - climbing into the center of the "Labyrinth". Just a trifling matter... Fine, I got it. I'll bring what you're asking for.
I'm very grateful to you, Professor! I'll take a walk for now...

Just when it seemed everything was ready to conduct the experiment to rid me of the Monolith's psychocode, another "little" detail surfaced. According to Sakharov, the energy blowback during contact could simply kill me. To calculate all the options and minimize the risk, he needs to experiment with the artifact that will act as an intermediary conductor in our scheme, i.e., the "Pendulum". To rule out any mistakes, the sample must be in perfect condition. Do I even need to mention that getting the artifact falls to me, of course.
You said you needed a "Pendulum" to thoroughly clarify everything about how it's safest for me to establish contact with the Monolith...
Yes, I did. Did you bring it? I hope I won't have any complaints regarding the condition of the sample?
You shouldn't. You took it out of the capsule yourself, and after that, a fly... didn't do anything to it. See for yourself.

Just a minute... Yes, the condition is perfect. Now I need to work with this artifact for a while. I will send you a message as soon as I obtain reliable results. Or you can drop by yourself tomorrow, around this same time.
Agreed, Professor.
Well, how are things, are the results from the "Pendulum" research ready?

Yes. And I must say, they fully confirmed my assumption regarding the risks of establishing contact with the Monolith. If the artifact is in its active phase or anti-phase at that moment, the probability of a lethal outcome from the energy blowback will be practically one hundred percent. But there is also good news...
Let me guess. There is another phase, not as dangerous for our scheme, right?

Correct. We customarily call it the "state of rest". It is an intermediate phase characterized by the complete absence of any manifestations of the anomalous nature of this object. In other words, both positive and negative properties are absent. The state of rest can be called conditionally safe for establishing contact with external information fields. I estimate the degree of risk at only 3-5%, which, you must agree, can be considered acceptable.

I suppose. Especially when you're not the one taking the risk, but someone else. Alright, that's just lyricism. Tell me the principle by which the phases alternate, and then let's go over my entire sequence of actions one last time.

I hate to disappoint you, young man, but I have not yet managed to identify the pattern of the phase alternation. You will have to determine that in practice yourself. I can only say with certainty that the change of phases is somehow connected to the current time, with each state lasting no less than an hour.\nAs for the sequence of actions... You arrive at the "Dead Monolith" with the "Pendulum" on your person. Activate it on your belt, having previously made sure that the artifact's current phase is the state of rest. Concentrate your attention on the object, focus. Tactile contact would be more than appropriate, I think, so it's best to touch the object. Well, that's essentially it - you clearly formulate your intention and mentally broadcast it to the recipient.

Oh, you're inimitable, Professor. Though, surprisingly, I understood everything. Guess I've already gotten used to your way of expressing yourself. Yeah, the last point, of course, is the hardest... Well, no big deal, I'll manage somehow. Thank you very much, I'm in your debt. Wish me luck.
Be sure to come back afterwards and tell me how it all went. Break a leg, Marked One!
Go to hell!

So, it seems everything is now ready to attempt purging the Monolith's psychocode from my consciousness. I will need a "Pendulum" in its neutral phase, devoid of any properties. If only I knew how these phases change. With it, I must go to the Foggy Thicket, to the "Dead Monolith". There, I need to activate the artifact on my belt and touch that strange huge crystal. After that, I must focus really well and broadcast my mental request to the Monolith. It doesn't sound too hard in theory, but as for how it will be in practice... Sakharov warned about the probability of a lethal outcome if I make a mistake choosing the current phase of the "Pendulum", but even with the right one, there's no 100% guarantee of success. However, it's too late to turn back. Forward, only forward!
